speaker: doctor
text: Good morning. From your initial vitals, I see your systolic blood pressure is 93 mmHg, which is on the lower side. Have you been experiencing any dizziness or fainting?

speaker: doctor
text: Based on your overall examination and history, it appears you have essential hypertension. We'll need to manage this carefully.

speaker: doctor
text: Your lab results show your hemoglobin is 178 g/L, which is slightly elevated. This might be related to your current condition.

speaker: patient
text: I've noticed that sometimes I feel a bit short of breath, especially when climbing stairs.

speaker: doctor
text: That shortness of breath could be related to your blood pressure and heart function. Let's keep monitoring that closely.

speaker: doctor
text: To help control your hypertension, I'm starting you on Lisinopril 20mg once daily. This medication should help lower your blood pressure effectively.

speaker: doctor
text: I also checked your heart rate, which is 85 beats per minute. That's within a reasonable range, but we'll keep an eye on it.

speaker: doctor
text: Your body temperature is 39.3 degrees Celsius, indicating you have a fever. Are you experiencing chills or any other symptoms?

speaker: patient
text: Yes, I've been feeling quite warm and have had some sweating at night.

speaker: doctor
text: Given your fever and hypertension, we should investigate for any possible infections or inflammations.

speaker: patient
text: I haven't noticed any cough or other symptoms, but I do feel generally unwell.

speaker: doctor
text: Understood. We'll run some more tests to rule out any underlying causes of the fever.

speaker: patient
text: Is this fever something that can be linked to my hypertension?

speaker: doctor
text: Not directly, but sometimes infections can complicate blood pressure control. It's important we treat both issues.

speaker: patient
text: I checked my blood pressure again today at home, and it was still 93 mmHg systolic.

speaker: patient
text: Yes, that reading of 93 mmHg I mentioned earlier has been consistent over the last few days.

speaker: doctor
text: Given the ongoing issues, I'm going to update your treatment and start you on Metformin 1000mg twice daily to help manage your blood sugar levels alongside your hypertension.

speaker: doctor
text: Also, I just received your latest temperature reading, and it has risen to 40.0 degrees Celsius, which is concerning. We need to act quickly.

speaker: doctor
text: Your latest hemoglobin has also increased slightly to 180 g/L, which we will monitor closely as it might indicate an underlying issue.

speaker: patient
text: My heart rate is still around 85 beats per minute, same as last time you measured it.
